original_query: SELECT "t0"."SAL" FROM (SELECT "EMPNO", "ENAME", "JOB", "MGR", "HIREDATE", "SAL", "COMM", "SLACKER", "SAL" AS "SAL0", "SAL" = 4 AS "f9" FROM "EMPNULLABLES" WHERE "DEPTNO" = 20 AND "SAL" > 1000) AS "t0" INNER JOIN (SELECT "t2"."SAL", "t5"."SAL" AS "SAL0", "t5"."f9" FROM (SELECT "EMPNO", "ENAME", "JOB", "MGR", "HIREDATE", "SAL", "COMM", "SLACKER" FROM "EMPNULLABLES" WHERE "DEPTNO" = 20 AND "SAL" > 1000) AS "t2" INNER JOIN (SELECT "SAL", "SAL" = 4 AS "f9" FROM "EMPNULLABLES" WHERE "DEPTNO" = 20 AND "SAL" > 1000 GROUP BY "SAL", "SAL" = 4) AS "t5" ON "t2"."SAL" = "t5"."SAL" OR "t5"."f9" GROUP BY "t2"."SAL", "t5"."SAL", "t5"."f9" HAVING "t5"."SAL" = "t2"."SAL") AS "t8" ON "t0"."SAL" = "t8"."SAL0" AND "t0"."f9" = "t8"."f9"

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Eliminated redundant subqueries and joins. The original query had multiple nested subqueries and joins that were not adding any filtering logic. All subqueries were selecting from the same table with identical WHERE conditions.
Simplified the query by removing unnecessary conditions and expressions. The complex join conditions and HAVING clauses were redundant as they didn't affect the final result set.
Removed all subqueries as they were not necessary for the query logic. The original query was effectively just selecting SAL from rows where DEPTNO=20 and SAL>1000.
